steps.title: Parallel lines have equal slopes — read slope directly from standard form
steps.content:
For $ax + by = c$ the slope is $-a/b$, no algebra needed.
Line $j$: $3x + 4y = 28$ → slope $= -3/4$.
Line $k$: $24x + ry = 24$ → slope $= -24/r$.
Set equal: $-3/4 = -24/r$ → $3r = 96$ → $r = 32$.

steps.title: Plug the y-intercept point into line k
steps.content:
Line $k$ is $24x + 32y = 24$. The point $(0, b)$ lies on it, so substitute $x = 0$:
$$32b = 24 \implies b = \frac{3}{4}$$
The answer is $\boxed{0.75}$ (or $3/4$).
